"cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-290", "name": "Authentication Bypass by Spoofing"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-345", "name": "Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity"}], "description": "### Impact

Puma is vulnerable to source IP spoofing when `set_remote_address proxy_protocol: :v1` is enabled and persistent connections are used.

PROXY protocol v1 is a connection-level protocol. [Support was added to Puma in v5.5.0](https://github.com/puma/puma/issues/2651). A proxy sends one PROXY header at the beginning of a TCP connection, before any HTTP data. Puma incorrectly re-parsed PROXY protocol headers after each keep-alive request on the same connection. An attacker able to send HTTP requests through a trusted proxy could therefore inject a second PROXY header between HTTP requests. Puma would treat the injected header as authoritative for the next request and overwrite `REMOTE_ADDR`.

This can mislead applications or middleware that use `REMOTE_ADDR` for security decisions, rate limiting, auditing, or allow/deny lists.

**Only deployments that explicitly enable PROXY protocol v1 are affected**, and will have set:

```ruby
set_remote_address proxy_protocol: :v1
```

Puma's default configuration is not affected. Deployments that do not use persistent connections to Puma are also not expected to be affected by this issue.

### Patches

Users should upgrade to versions 7.2.1 or 8.0.2.

### Workarounds

Disable PROXY protocol v1 parsing if it is not required:

 ```ruby
   # remove/comment this:
   # set_remote_address proxy_protocol: :v1
 ```

Users can also disable persistent connections to Puma, for example:

```ruby
enable_keep_alives false
```

### References

- [HAProxy PROXY protocol specification](https://www.haproxy.org/download/1.8/doc/proxy-protocol.txt)
- [CVE-2025-31135 / GHSA-c2c3-pqw5-5p7c: go-guerrilla repeated PROXY command source IP spoofing](https://github.com/phires/go-guerrilla/security/advisories/GHSA-c2c3-pqw5-5p7c)
- [Puma `set_remote_address` documentation](https://github.com/puma/puma/blob/master/lib/puma/dsl.rb)", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00177, "percentile": 0.07401}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-2vqw-3mp8-cgmx", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-09T00:09:44Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2vqw-3mp8-cgmx", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-2vqw-3mp8-cgmx"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-47737"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-09T00:09:44Z", "references": ["https://github.com/puma/puma/security/advisories/GHSA-2vqw-3mp8-cgmx", "https://github.com/rubysec/ruby-advisory-db/blob/master/gems/puma/CVE-2026-47737.yml", "https://www.cve.org/CVERecord/SearchResults?query=CVE-2026-47737", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2vqw-3mp8-cgmx"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/puma/puma/security-advisories/GHSA-2vqw-3mp8-cgmx", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/puma/puma", "summary": "Puma PROXY Protocol v1 Accepts Repeated Protocol Headers on Persistent Connections", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-09T00:09:45Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-2vqw-3mp8-cgmx", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "8.0.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "rubygems", "name": "puma"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 8.0.0, < 8.0.2"}, {"first_patched_version": "7.2.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "rubygems", "name": "puma"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 5.5.0, < 7.2.1"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Puma PROXY Protocol v1 Accepts Repeated Protocol Headers on Persistent Connections
